b'
Hi, and welcome to Line Sheet. Happy first day of work for one of our favorite designers at a place that still won\xe2\x80\x99t acknowledge he\xe2\x80\x99s working there! Before we get started: I wanted this to be a Kanye West\xe2\x80\x93free zone today, but his actions this weekend\xe2\x80\x94most notably, putting a t-shirt with a swastika up for sale on Yeezy.com\xe2\x80\x94have been utterly bewildering and frankly frightening.

Feb 10, 2025

Lauren Sherman

Hi, and welcome to Line Sheet. Happy first day of work for one of our favorite designers at a place that still won\xe2\x80\x99t acknowledge he\xe2\x80\x99s working there!

Before we get started: I wanted this to be a Kanye West \xe2\x80\x93free zone today, but his actions this weekend\xe2\x80\x94most notably, putting a t-shirt with a swastika up for sale on Yeezy.com\xe2\x80\x94have been utterly bewildering and frankly frightening. I may address this further tomorrow, but I\xe2\x80\x99ll be watching how Shopify, Yeezy\xe2\x80\x99s e-commerce platform, responds to this.

Anyway, in less torturous news, this New York Fashion Week has been so um, chill , that I paid attention to the Super Bowl. (As in, I looked at highlights online after having dinner at the Odeon. Are there other restaurants in New York? I wouldn\xe2\x80\x99t know because this is the only one where I can get a reservation without asking a P.R. person for help.) It may be a controversial take, but I\xe2\x80\x99m happy for all of us that Kendrick Lamar performed the halftime show. This is the positive side of the dissolution of monoculture. (Apparently, the president left before the end of the first half.)


I also have some thoughts on what went down\xe2\x80\x94fashion-wise, at least\xe2\x80\x94in New Orleans, plus an update on the Gucci situation. And yes, I offer my opinion on Calvin Klein and the rest of the Old-Old C\xc3\xa9line brands. (Speaking of, I heard from some insiders that whatever Michael Rider is up to over at New-New Celine, it\xe2\x80\x99s looking very good.)

\xf0\x9f\x9a\xa8\xf0\x9f\x9a\xa8 Programming note : Tomorrow on Fashion People , Harper\xe2\x80\x99s Bazaar executive editor Leah Chernikoff joins me to chat about what\xe2\x80\x99s happening in New York (from Christopher John Rogers to Coach), Kendrick\xe2\x80\x99s ( Hedi -designed) Celine jeans, and Gucci. Listen here and here .


Mentioned in this issue : Calvin Klein , Kate Moss , Veronica Leoni , Eva Serrano , PVH, Stefan Larsson , Christy Turlington , Kendrick Lamar , bootcut jeans, Old-Old C\xc3\xa9line, Gucci, Sabato De Sarno , Hedi Slimane , Raf Simons , Prada, Chanel, Helmut Lang , Jil Sander, Dior, Phoebe Philo , The Row, and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

The all-electric BMW i7 merges technology and design with an available Panoramic Sky Lounge LED Roof and 4D surround sound. Learn more at BMWUSA.com .

Three Things You Should Know...

  • The Hedi factor and Sabato severance : In Thursday\xe2\x80\x99s Inner Circle issue , I offered a pretty clear outline of what happened at Gucci last week, including details on how Sabato De Sarno \xe2\x80\x99s last day went down. (It\xe2\x80\x99s not too late to upgrade your account and read it.) There is still a question of what Kering will do next, with speculation in Milan leaning toward Hedi Slimane rejoining the group for a down-and-dirty revamp. Don\xe2\x80\x99t count out Maria Grazia Chiuri , but Slimane is the most obvious choice if Kering wants a swift course correction\xe2\x80\x94even if there was some drama when he quit Saint Laurent almost a decade ago. All can be forgiven when there is money to be made, and Kering absolutely must nail this transition in order to restore investor confidence.

    I keep getting asked about De Sarno\xe2\x80\x99s payout. There\xe2\x80\x99s been a lot of online speculation about how much he\xe2\x80\x99s getting, but I\xe2\x80\x99m not even going to write out the number that people are suggesting because it seems so, so off base to me. Here\xe2\x80\x99s what I know: These initial contracts are typically three years long, and he didn\xe2\x80\x99t make it into year two, so this is going to come down to the lawyers. It\xe2\x80\x99s worth remembering, though, that De Sarno was an unknown quantity when he arrived at Gucci, and his initial salary was far, far lower than that of someone with a proven track record. Some of these big designers make $10 million, or even $20 million, a year. Probably not De Sarno. It\xe2\x80\x99s also likely that they haven\xe2\x80\x99t even worked out the terms of his exit yet.
  • What happens when you\xe2\x80\x99ve had a lot of head injuries and you love to get dressed : I\xe2\x80\x99m interested in how NFL players have followed in the footsteps of their more worldly counterparts in the NBA by embracing fashion. Sunday\xe2\x80\x99s Super Bowl tunnel walk in New Orleans was pretty spectacular, from Travis Kelce \xe2\x80\x99s Amiri rust-colored suit, straight out of American Hustle , to A.J. Brown \xe2\x80\x99s leather tie. My favorite, though, was DeAndre Hopkins \xe2\x80\x99s mink coat. The hokiness of a sponcon party aside, I also totally get why Emily Bode decided to stage a runway show at GQ \xe2\x80\x99s pre-gamer on Friday night at Hotel Peter and Paul in New Orleans. Much better for business than opening New York Fashion Week.

    Of course, the WAGs in this scenario are just as important, since they are led by Taylor Swift , who showed up at the game with the Haim sisters in white thigh-high boots, jorts, a white tank top, and an off-white blazer, which she quickly lost given the temperature in the stadium. This was, objectively, the worst thing she\xe2\x80\x99s worn in ages, although exactly right for the moment. Once again, she\xe2\x80\x99s cosplaying as the college student dating the football star.

    It\xe2\x80\x99s easy to blame her poor choices on the return of Britney -and- Justin -era Y2K fashion, but there is always a subset of clothing obsessives who are going to dress in a gaggy way, and it just so happens that the look reflects the crassness in our current culture. \xe2\x80\x9cA very MAGA experience\xe2\x80\x9d is how one friend, who is married to an Eagles fan (I\xe2\x80\x99m relieved for her), responded to my disdain for the outfits worn by Swift and friends.

    What I didn\xe2\x80\x99t hate were Kendrick Lamar\xe2\x80\x99s Celine bootcut jeans, which went viral in the hours following the halftime show, raising concerns about whether the men of America would be forced out of their Rag & Bone Fit 2s . (If you\xe2\x80\x99re still wearing the Fit 1 \xe2\x80\xa6 best of luck to you.) As we know, fashion cycles are murkier than they used to be because of how we receive information, and Prada just showed tight-around-the-ankle trousers to confuse us even further.

    I would argue that Lamar\xe2\x80\x99s bootcut is bound to influence women more than men. My friends and I are increasingly reaching for Levi\xe2\x80\x99s 517s instead of 501s and wearing them with pointy shoes, in the style of a Murray Hill girl. There\xe2\x80\x99s a lot more to say about Lamar\xe2\x80\x99s look, including his Martine Rose\xe2\x80\x93custom jacket, but all I really know is that the biggest American fashion moment of the week happened 1,300 miles away from Fashion Week.
  • Anti-mood-board brands : My friend Poppy has a term for the brands that only reference other brands: mood board brands , the most cutting and accurate description of most of what is offered to consumers. I\xe2\x80\x99m down with a well-executed mood board brand: The best of them, like Toteme and Sporty & Rich, are so good that they end up becoming reference points themselves.

    At their show on Saturday, Eckhaus Latta\xe2\x80\x99s Mike Eckhaus and Zoe Latta chose to address this issue head-on with Vogue \xe2\x80\x99s Laia Garcia-Furtado . What they do is \xe2\x80\x9cnot based on a mood board,\xe2\x80\x9d Latta told Laia. \xe2\x80\x9cThis is based on how we see the world.\xe2\x80\x9d And indeed, their view is truly singular: simple, sexy, and chock-full of great jeans\xe2\x80\x94but also a reflection of how vile the world can absolutely be. (I loved the faded grey rugby, its stripes created by piecing together old jerseys. They paired it with long leather gloves\xe2\x80\x94base and elegant at once.)

Photo: Madison Voelkel/BFA/Courtesy of Eckhaus Latta

  • It\xe2\x80\x99s amazing how much Eckhaus and Latta can do with very little. I thought the same at Christopher John Rogers, who showed for the first time in ages on Thursday night in the Agger Fish Building at the Brooklyn Navy Yard. I have always admired Rogers for his expert use of color, and his unwillingness to bend to the will of the mood boards, but the years of working with clients has made him far more precise. It\xe2\x80\x99s so nice to see progression.

    On the big-money front, the two best-in-class shows are Ulla Johnson and Khaite. At Ulla on Sunday morning, there were so many buyers. Why? Because Johnson \xe2\x80\x99s accordion-pleat paisleys and lush purple overcoats hit the mark for a certain customer, and evolve as she evolves. I was similarly impressed at Catherine Holstein \xe2\x80\x99s Park Avenue show on Saturday night. The Holstein-curated soundtrack, per usual, punched above New York\xe2\x80\x99s weight. (My friend said he streams it every season just to hear what she chooses; last night\xe2\x80\x99s highlights were \xe2\x80\x9cLuna\xe2\x80\x9d by the Smashing Pumpkins and \xe2\x80\x9cAngel\xe2\x80\x9d by Sonic Boom.)

    But I\xe2\x80\x99d say the clothes did, too. Yes, the circular, raised staging was grand, and the page-boy-cap styling was dramatic. The hats needed to go. And yet, these were easy clothes for women who just want a G.D. leopard print coat. Holstein is a million times better at this than 95 percent of the designers currently leading houses in Milan and Paris. Sometimes, I wish she would take one of those jobs and tackle a brand with history, if only to prove her critics wrong.

And now, on to the main event\xe2\x80\xa6

The Calvin Past-Present Conundrum

Calvin Klein\xe2\x80\x99s much-hyped runway show, its first in seven years, celebrated the brand\xe2\x80\x99s minimalist history. But was it anything more than a splashy retrospective?

Lauren Sherman

Technically speaking, the Calvin Klein runway show that took place early Friday afternoon at the company\xe2\x80\x99s headquarters off Seventh Avenue happened . Yes, it happened in the sense that there were celebrities in the front row and that the 82-year-old Calvin Klein was there, posing with his ex and longtime muse, Kelly Klein , alongside Kate Moss and Christy Turlington . And it happened in the sense that clothes came down a runway. It also happened in the sense that editors flew in from all over the world to support Veronica Leoni , the new creative director, who is based in Rome and firmly established in the European network.

Photo: Courtesy of Calvin Klein Collection

But the Calvin Klein Collection\xe2\x80\x94with its new clothing tag, fabulously redesigned by M/M Paris in the vein of the original 1970s style\xe2\x80\x94felt less like a new collection and more like a retrospective of minimalism from the past 40 years: pieces directly referencing Calvin archives, the Helmut Lang archives, and Leoni\xe2\x80\x99s own archives. After all, she has worked at Jil Sander, Phoebe Philo \xe2\x80\x99s C\xc3\xa9line, and The Row, and you could see that she was claiming certain silhouettes from her time spent at those places as her own, down to her take on Philo\xe2\x80\x99s signature Box bag.


Leoni\xe2\x80\x99s debut was, however, a new day for PVH, Calvin Klein\xe2\x80\x99s parent company, which is much more invested in the business of jeans and t-shirts and underwear, often via a partner, than designer clothes. For years, the ready-to-wear collection, designed after Klein\xe2\x80\x99s retirement by Francesco Costa , was barely sold beyond the brand\xe2\x80\x99s former flagship store, a sterile white box on the corner of 60th and Madison. When Raf Simons took over, in 2016, Calvin Klein management pivoted to a wider distribution model, but the results were uninspiring, at least financially. Despite the spectacular reviews and industry ardor\xe2\x80\x94and an exuberant, Sterling Ruby-inflected redesign of the sterile white box\xe2\x80\x94making clothes and accessories in Italy is different from making underwear in Vietnam. The experiment failed, with Simons and his team\xe2\x80\x94 Pieter Mulier and Matthieu Blazy \xe2\x80\x94out after less than two years.

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

The all-electric BMW i7 merges technology and design with an available Panoramic Sky Lounge LED Roof and 4D surround sound. Learn more at BMWUSA.com .

These days, I assume that PVH C.E.O. Stefan Larsson and Calvin Klein brand president Eva Serrano , who both hail from the world of fast fashion, are aiming for something in between their traditional comfort zone and the former management team\xe2\x80\x99s experiments with the runway. Yes, there is a line sheet being passed around, but you\xe2\x80\x99re not going to see the Calvin Klein Collection in every specialty boutique across the globe, either. The collection goes to market in Paris in March, and retailers including Mytheresa, The Webster, Dover Street Market, and Selfridges have been invited to attend.

This show, from my view, was a very public test-and-learn situation that, in any other era, would not have been up for so much scrutiny.

The O.O.C.

PVH, after all, has plenty of billion-dollar problems to solve outside of Calvin Klein. Its license with G-III, which makes its women\xe2\x80\x99s apparel for Calvin Klein and Tommy Hilfiger, ends in 2027, and while PVH has been public about wanting to bring that very large part of the business in-house, it\xe2\x80\x99s not as easy as it sounds. Then there\xe2\x80\x99s the more pressing matter of Trump \xe2\x80\x99s trade war with China, which has put PVH on the country\xe2\x80\x99s \xe2\x80\x9cunreliable entities\xe2\x80\x9d list, essentially blacklisting the company from doing business there. (China sales make up about 6 percent of PVH\xe2\x80\x99s overall revenue, but 16 percent of profits.)

There\xe2\x80\x99s an argument that Larsson and Serrano shouldn\xe2\x80\x99t be relaunching ready-to-wear altogether: If the previous regime couldn\xe2\x80\x99t make it work with the current designers of Prada, Ala\xc3\xafa, and Chanel , how can anybody? But Leoni was a big hire, and the internet will be waiting for this collection to land on Ssense. And a runway show still offers a singular marketing opportunity that can\xe2\x80\x99t be supplanted by Jeremy Allen White in his underwear.


But Calvin Klein doesn\xe2\x80\x99t have a marketing problem; it has a product problem. People wear the underwear out of habit, but it\xe2\x80\x99s been ages since the jeans were top of mind. Did Leoni\xe2\x80\x99s show on Friday manage to change that? Well, a first collection should be considered as such, and Leoni\xe2\x80\x99s job is much harder at a place like PVH than at a luxury goods firm. Funnily enough, though, the pieces I liked most from Leoni\xe2\x80\x99s debut collection were the ones pulled from Simons\xe2\x80\x99s Calvin: the plaid shirts, denim, and cowboy boots. In The New York Times , Leoni promised sexiness, and this was the closest she got.

The problem with presenting a history book as a first collection, however, is that Leoni should have taken a stab at writing another chapter. I can\xe2\x80\x99t say I disliked any of it, really. (As a consumer of all of the brands she was referencing, I\xe2\x80\x99m the target audience.) But there wasn\xe2\x80\x99t anything I couldn\xe2\x80\x99t find elsewhere, from the collarless blazers to the \xe2\x80\x9980s pumps. I hope next season we\xe2\x80\x99ll find out what she really thinks.

The biggest issue I see in New York fashion is an obsession with Old-Old C\xc3\xa9line, Leoni\xe2\x80\x99s former stomping ground. (Let\xe2\x80\x99s call it O.O.C., because it\xe2\x80\x99s also out of control.) Phoebe Philo herself has moved on, and yet everyone is still making scarf coats. I have no problem with designers copying others\xe2\x80\x94Calvin Klein used to get accused of copying Helmut Lang , ya know\xe2\x80\x94but you have to make it your own. There were too many examples of this over the past few days: Right after the Calvin show, I headed to Fforme, where new designer Frances Howie is trying to loosen up one of these resolutely O.O.C. brands. She\xe2\x80\x99s on a better track than her predecessor, Paul Helbers , whose meticulous designs were too stiff, and there were pieces that\xe2\x80\x99ll move on the shop floor (the crinkled silks). Yet I left feeling unsure of why this needed to exist. (Again, this was a debut, so let\xe2\x80\x99s give her another season to convince us.)

Photo: Jonas Gustavsson/Courtesy of Ashlynn Park

Ashlynn Park , on the other hand, had no trouble convincing me at her show on Friday afternoon in the Cristina Grajales Gallery in Tribeca. Wow, what a fabulous collection by someone who has paid her dues. Park did time at Yohji Yamamoto, Alexander Wang, and, more recently, with Simons at Calvin Klein, before finally launching her own brand in 2020. Unlike everything else I saw on Friday, Park\xe2\x80\x99s collection felt like it was for the O.O.C. customer, but without the weight of that association. I loved the sculpted knits\xe2\x80\x94her signature\xe2\x80\x94and the way she layered them. There was so little this week that elicited desire, whereas Park\xe2\x80\x99s ribbed wools and baby-doll camisoles were so good I felt compelled to mention them to every person I could.

What I\xe2\x80\x99m Reading\xe2\x80\xa6 and Watching\xe2\x80\xa6

I was interviewed for the new issue of System magazine, which was a very meta experience. I first met with editor Jonathan Wingfield in June 2024, and by the autumn, he and his cofounders didn\xe2\x80\x99t work at System anymore. For several months, I was writing about their battle for the magazine, disclosing information I\xe2\x80\x99m sure no one involved wanted me to divulge. In the end, Wingfield, Elizabeth von Guttman , and Thomas Lenthal won the fight, and I did a second interview. They let me do my job, and I let them do theirs. I appreciate their interest! [System]

Pieter Mulier partnered with photographer Anthony Seklaoui to release a book of imagery documenting his time at Ala\xc3\xafa, all the way through the Guggenheim show. (On Thursday, February 13, in Paris, they\xe2\x80\x99re hosting a book signing at the Saint-Honor\xc3\xa9 store.) For collectors, Mulier\xe2\x80\x99s moment at Ala\xc3\xafa is unique in the history of fashion, and I suspect the value of this actual tome will appreciate fast. [ Harper\xe2\x80\x99s Bazaar ]

My kid loves R.E.M., so I secretly wish they\xe2\x80\x99d do the never-gonna-happen reunion, but I also think Will Leitch \xe2\x80\x99s take on why they won\xe2\x80\x99t is a good lesson for fashion designers and executives who continue harping on the past. Getting the band back together almost never works. [ WaPo ]


Soho House\xe2\x80\x99s future is in secondary and tertiary cities, where there aren\xe2\x80\x99t a ton of options. They should really be looking at the Hillstone Restaurant Group for guidance on how to execute this type of hospitality play at scale. [ The Wall Street Journal ]

And finally \xe2\x80\xa6 Top reader nominees for this year\xe2\x80\x99s American Vogue guest editor include Tory Burch , Beyonc\xc3\xa9 and Solange , Megan Thee Stallion , Carine Roitfeld , and\xe2\x80\xa6 Edward Enninful .

Until tomorrow,

Lauren


P.S .: We are using affiliate links because we are a business. We may make a couple bucks off them.
